rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible feature names or explicitly defined constants/variables in AGREE expressions; avoid direct references to AADL properties.
generic_rules: Contracts should express behavioral constraints using named, typed interfaces or defined constants, not implicit model properties.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference symbols that are lexically visible within the annex's owning component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'annex agree {** ... **};' to wrap all AGREE content.
Declare constants with 'const name : type = value;' and equations with 'eq name : type = expression;'.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions without explicit port or constant mapping.
Avoid placing implementation-specific assignments in component type annexes.
